rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Assignment Order And To Restrain The Debtors From Transferring Rents
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBINS MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT ORDER AND TO RESTRAIN THE DEBTORS FROM TRANSFERRING RENTS is DENIED. No evidence provided that California properties currently belong to the defendants. The Court declines plaintiff's invitation to create new equitable remedies for purported fraudulent transfers. Remedy at law exists. Plaintiff failed to address Defendants' jurisdictional argument regarding Texas property in reply. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EAH INC. VS. VIVIAN POY HONG LEE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2024 line 10. PLAINTIFF EAH INC. DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer to affirmative defenses for failure to state a claim and uncertainty is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. These are not affirmative defenses, but as acknowledged by the defendant in the Answer, bases for demurrer per CCP 430.10. Demurrer to the affirmative defenses for discrimination, retaliation, good cause, justification and excuse, breach of warranty of habitability/failure to repair, and violation of law is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as requested in the supplemental brief. Demurrer to the affirmative defenses for laches, breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment, waiver, estoppel, failure to mitigate, no injury, unclean hands, breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing, duty of care and conditions precedent not met is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Defendant failed to state facts in support of these purported affirmative defenses and did not seek leave to amend in either the opposition or the supplemental brief. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
